merger_name: CVC Capital Partners – The animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CVC Capital Partners plc. (CVC Capital Partners) proposes to
acquire the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-
Firmenich Group as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition consists of:
• CVC Capital Partners to indirectly acquire, through Specialty
Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco B.V.,
and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., an 80% shareholding and
sole control over the SpecialtyCo Business and EssentialCo
Business (which together comprise the animal nutrition and
health division), and
• the DSM-Firmenich Group to (indirectly through to be
incorporated entities) reinvest in each of the SpecialityCo
Business and the EssentialCo Business to retain a ~20%
non-controlling, minority stake in each.
Specialty Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco
B.V., and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc applied for a notification waiver
in respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, CVC Capital Partners, is a global alternative investment
manager focused on private equity, credit, infrastructure and
secondaries. CVC Capital Partners hold interests in companies
active in a variety of industries around the world, including in
financial services, chemicals, utilities, manufacturing, retailing and
distribution, primarily in Europe, the US and the Asia-Pacific region.
The target, the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-
Firmenich Group, is a global provider of science-based animal
nutrition and health solutions. Its core business is the manufacture
and supply of animal nutrition ingredients, particularly vitamins and
carotenoids, for livestock and aquaculture feed. It is also involved in
the premix industry, which serve as a link from feed additives to final
feed composition in the animal nutrition value chain.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap
between the products and services supplied by the animal nutrition
and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group and CVC Capital
Partners controlled portfolio companies in Australia, or any vertical
relationships.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
